questions.question: Which Indian has won the USIBC (US-India Business Council) Global Leadership Award?
questions.explanation: The Chairman of Tata Group N Chandrasekaran is selected for the US-India Business Council, USIBC Global Leadership Award this year. Along with him, the CEO of Lockheed Martin, Jim Taiclet will also receive the award. The USIBC Global Leadership Awards are conferred on executives to recognise their leadership and their contribution in promoting trade and business between India and the United States.

questions.question: India posted a trade surplus of USD 790 million in which month of 2020, after over 18 years?
questions.explanation: As per the recent data by the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, India posted a trade surplus of USD 790 million in June 2020. This surplus has been recorded for the first time after over 18 years. Due to the Covid-19 induced decrease in domestic demand for crude oil, gold and other industrial products, the country’s import was deeply reduced. This led to a trade surplus with imports of USD 21.11 billion and exports of USD 21.91 billion.

questions.question: What is the rank of India, in the Global Manufacturing Risk Index (MRI), released by Cushman & Wakefield?
questions.explanation: As per the recent report released by the property consultant Cushman & Wakefield, India ranks third in the list of most-suitable locations for global manufacturing, in terms of cost competitiveness and operating conditions. Among 48 countries, China and the US bagged the top two positions of the Global Manufacturing Risk Index (MRI). Last year, India was in the fourth position in the list, which ranks 48 countries in Europe, America and the Asia-Pacific.

questions.question: Which state has announced that its Covid-19 clusters have more than 50% of Community transmission?
questions.explanation: The Health Minister of Kerala KK Shailaja has recently announced that the rate of community transmission in Covid-19 clusters in the state is more than 50%. In the 84 clusters in the state, local transmission is more than 50%. However, the rate of transmission is less than 10% outside the clusters. The Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan also confirmed that community transmission was detected in two coastal villages of Thiruvananthapuram.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry has established an ‘Investment Clearance Cell’, for easier clearance of proposals?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

questions.explanation: Union Ministry of Civil Aviation has recently established an Investment Clearance Cell (ICC), aimed at providing assistance and clearance of proposals. The ICC will comprise of ten members and it will be headed by a Joint Secretary in the Ministry. The cell will be a single-window system for attracting investments and it will work closely with states to rope in investors.

questions.question: John Lewis, who passed away recently, was associated with the civil rights movement of which country?
questions.explanation: John Lewis, who was a pioneer of the civil rights movement of the United States, recently passed away at the age of 80. He entered the United States House of Representatives in the year 1986 from Atlanta and he was a long-time member of the Congress. He was praised as the “Conscience of the Congress” for his sincere and conscious traits.

questions.question: Which country has launched its spacecraft to Mars named ‘Hope’?
questions.explanation: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has become the first Arab country to launch a spacecraft to Mars. In its first interplanetary mission, it has sent a car-sized spacecraft named ‘Hope’. UAE has sent the spacecraft from the space centre of Japan. After traversing the space, it will start orbiting around Mars to examine its weather. China is set to launch its Mars Mission on July 23. NASA is also to launch its Perseverance Mars rover to examine its hospitability.

questions.question: What is the rank of India in the Global Direct Selling –2019 Retail Sales report?
questions.explanation: The World Federation of Direct Selling Associations (WFDSA) has released the Global Direct Selling –2019 Retail Sales report. As per the latest edition of the report, India’s direct selling industry has recorded sales of USD 2.47 billion in 2019, reporting a growth of 12.1 per cent. Due to the growth in sales, India has been ranked 15th in the report, from its previous year rank of 19. The list is topped by the US. In terms of the number of direct sellers, India is at 6th rank.

questions.question: Which Union Ministry has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) for data sharing?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MoMSME) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) for sharing of data. Under the MoU, certain Income-tax Return (ITR) related information will be seamlessly shared by the Income Tax Department to Ministry of MSME. With this data, the Ministry will be able to check and classify the enterprises, as per the MSME category.

questions.question: Which Indian-origin nurse has been conferred with the Singapore President’s Award for COVID-19 Services?
questions.explanation: Kala Narayanasamy, a 59-year-old Indian-origin nurse in Singapore has been conferred with the President’s Award for Nurses. She has been awarded for serving on the frontline during the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly for using infection control practices learnt during SARS outbreak in 2003. The 10,000 SGD -award is given to 5 nurses for their services.
